hierarchy.title: RESERVE BANK OF INDIA
hierarchy.text:
RESERVE BANK OF INDIA
www.rbi.org.in
RBI/2021-22/79


FMRD.DIRD.03/14.01.003/2021-22 June 4, 2021

To,

All Eligible Market Participants

Madam/Sir,
Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Certificate of Deposit) Directions, 2021

Please refer to Paragraph 6 of the Statement on Developmental and Regulatory Policies,
announced as a part of the second Bi-monthly Monetary Policy Statement for 2019-20 dated June
06, 2019 regarding Comprehensive Review of Money Market Directions. A reference is also
invited to Paragraph 5 of the Statement on Developmental and Regulatory Policies, announced
as a part of the second Bi-monthly Monetary Policy Statement for 2021-22 dated June 04, 2021 on
'Facilitating Flexibility in Liquidity Management by issuers of Certificates of Deposit'.

hierarchy.title: 2. The draft Directions on Certificate of Deposits were released for public comments on December
hierarchy.text:
2. The draft Directions on Certificate of Deposits were released for public comments on December
04, 2020. Based on the feedback received from the market participants, the Reserve Bank of
India (Certificate of Deposit) Directions, 2021 were reviewed and have since been finalised.
The Directions are enclosed herewith.
Yours faithfully,
(Dimple Bhandia) Chief General Manager

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
FINANCIAL MARKETS REGULATION DEPARTMENT
Notification No. FMRD.DIRD.04/14.01.003/2021-22 dated June 04, 2021
Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Certificate of Deposit) Directions, 2021
In exercise of the powers conferred under section 45W of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934
(hereinafter called the Act) read with section 45U of the Act and of all the powers enabling it in
this behalf and in supersession of Section III of FMRD.Master Direction No. 2/2016-17 dated July
07, 2016, the Reserve Bank of India (hereinafter called the Reserve Bank) hereby issues the
following Directions to all persons and agencies eligible to deal in Certificate of Deposit.

children.title: (a) These Directions shall be called the Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Certificate
children.text:
(a) These Directions shall be called the Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Certificate
of Deposit) Directions, 2021.

children.title: (b) These Directions shall come into force with effect from June 07, 2021.
children.text: (b) These Directions shall come into force with effect from June 07, 2021.

children.title: (a) For the purpose of these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires:
children.text: (a) For the purpose of these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires:

children.title: (i) "Bank" means a banking company (including a Payment Bank and a Small Finance
children.text:
(i) "Bank" means a banking company (including a Payment Bank and a Small Finance
Bank) as defined in clause (c) of section 5 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or a
"regional rural bank", a "corresponding new bank" or "State Bank of India" as defined
in clauses (ja), (da) and (nc), of section 5 respectively thereof, or a "cooperative bank"
as defined in clause (cci) of section 5 read with section 56 of the said Act;

children.title: (ii) "Benchmark Interest Rates" means interest rates administered by Financial
children.text:
(ii) "Benchmark Interest Rates" means interest rates administered by Financial
Benchmark Administrators;

children.title: (iii) "Certificate of Deposit" or "CD" is a negotiable, unsecured money market
(iv) "Delivery versus Payment" or "DvP" means a settlement mechanism which
(v) "Depository" shall have the meaning assigned in section 2 (e) of the Depositories
children.text:
(v) "Depository" shall have the meaning assigned in section 2 (e) of the Depositories
Act, 1996 (22 of 1996);

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (vi) "Electronic Trading Platform" or "ETP" shall have the meaning assigned in
(vii) "Financial Benchmark Administrator" or "FBA" means a person who controls
(viii) "Over-the-Counter markets" or "OTC markets" refers to markets where
(ix) "Person resident in India" shall have the same meaning assigned to it in section 2
children.text: (ix) "Person resident in India" shall have the same meaning assigned to it in section 2

children.title: (v) of the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999;
children.text: (v) of the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999;

children.title: (x) "Recognised stock exchanges" shall have the meaning assigned in section 2 (f) of
children.text:
(x) "Recognised stock exchanges" shall have the meaning assigned in section 2 (f) of
the Securities Contract Regulation Act, 1956;

children.title: (xi) "Small Finance Bank" means a bank licensed under section 22 of the Banking
children.text:
(xi) "Small Finance Bank" means a bank licensed under section 22 of the Banking
Regulation Act, 1949 and governed by the terms of the "Reserve Bank Guidelines for
Licensing of Small Finance Banks" dated November 27, 2014, as amended from time
to time.

children.title: (b) Words and expressions used but not defined in these Directions shall have the meaning
children.text:
(b) Words and expressions used but not defined in these Directions shall have the meaning
assigned to them in the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

children.title: (a) Certificate of Deposits (CDs) may be issued by:
children.text: (a) Certificate of Deposits (CDs) may be issued by:

children.title: (i) Scheduled Commercial Banks;
children.text: (i) Scheduled Commercial Banks;

children.title: (ii) Regional Rural Banks; and
children.text: (ii) Regional Rural Banks; and

children.title: (iii) Small Finance Banks.
children.text: (iii) Small Finance Banks.

children.title: (b) CDs issued by the All India Financial Institution shall be guided by the Directions contained
children.text:
(b) CDs issued by the All India Financial Institution shall be guided by the Directions contained
in Master Circular No. FID.FIC.1/01.02.00/2015-16 issued by the Reserve Bank on
Resource Raising Norms for Financial Institutions dated July 01, 2015, as amended from
time to time.

hierarchy.title: 4. Eligible investors
hierarchy.text:
4. Eligible investors
CDs may be issued to all persons resident in India.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (a) Primary issuance
children.text: (a) Primary issuance

children.title: (i) CDs shall be issued only in dematerialised form and held with a depository registered
children.text:
(i) CDs shall be issued only in dematerialised form and held with a depository registered
with Securities and Exchange Board of India.

children.title: (ii) CDs shall be issued in minimum denomination of ₹5 lakh and in multiples of ₹5 lakh
children.text:
(ii) CDs shall be issued in minimum denomination of ₹5 lakh and in multiples of ₹5 lakh
thereafter.

children.title: (iii) The tenor of a CD at issuance shall not be less than seven days and shall not exceed
children.text:
(iii) The tenor of a CD at issuance shall not be less than seven days and shall not exceed
one year.

children.title: (iv) CDs shall be issued on a T+1 basis where T represents the date of closure of the
children.text:
(iv) CDs shall be issued on a T+1 basis where T represents the date of closure of the
offer period for issuance of the CDs.

children.title: (b) Discount/coupon rate
children.text:
(b) Discount/coupon rate
CDs may be issued at a discount to the face value. CDs may also be issued on a fixed /
floating rate basis provided the interest rate on the floating rate CD is reset at periodic
rests agreed to at the time of issue and is linked to a benchmark published by a Financial
Benchmark Administrator or approved by the Fixed Income Money Market and Derivatives
Association of India (FIMMDA) for this purpose. FIMMDA shall ensure that any floating
rate approved by them for this purpose is determined transparently, objectively and in
arm's length transactions.

children.title: (c) Secondary market - trading venues and settlement
children.text: (c) Secondary market - trading venues and settlement

children.title: (i) CDs shall be traded either in Over-the-Counter (OTC) markets, including on Electronic
children.text:
(i) CDs shall be traded either in Over-the-Counter (OTC) markets, including on Electronic
Trading Platforms, or on recognised stock exchanges with the approval of the
Reserve Bank.

children.title: (ii) The settlement cycle for OTC trades in CDs shall be T+0 or T+1.
children.text: (ii) The settlement cycle for OTC trades in CDs shall be T+0 or T+1.

children.title: (iii) All secondary market transactions in CDs shall be settled on a DvP basis through the
children.text:
(iii) All secondary market transactions in CDs shall be settled on a DvP basis through the
clearing corporation of any recognized stock exchange or any other mechanism
approved by the Reserve Bank.

children.title: (d) Loans against CDs
children.text:
(d) Loans against CDs
Banks are not allowed to grant loans against CDs, unless specifically permitted by the
Reserve Bank.

children.title: (e) Buyback of CDs
children.text:
(e) Buyback of CDs
Issuing banks are permitted to buyback CDs before maturity. Buyback of CDs shall be
subject to the following conditions:

children.title: (i) Buyback of CDs can be made only 7 days after the date of issue of the CD;
children.text: (i) Buyback of CDs can be made only 7 days after the date of issue of the CD;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (ii) The buyback offer shall be made to all investors in a particular CD issue on identical
(iii) Buyback of CDs shall be at the prevailing market price; and
children.text: (iii) Buyback of CDs shall be at the prevailing market price; and

children.title: (iv) CDs bought back, partially or in full, shall be extinguished.
children.text: (iv) CDs bought back, partially or in full, shall be extinguished.

children.title: (f) Market timings
children.text:
(f) Market timings
Primary issuance and secondary market trading hours shall be between 9:00 AM and 5:00
PM on a business day or as specified by the Reserve Bank from time to time.

children.title: (g) Repayment of CD
children.text:
(g) Repayment of CD
There will be no grace period for repayment of CDs.

children.title: (h) Market practices and documentation
children.text:
(h) Market practices and documentation
Eligible participants and agencies in the CD market shall follow the standardised
procedures and documentation which may be prescribed by FIMMDA, in consultation with
the Reserve Bank, for operational flexibility and smooth functioning of the markets.

children.title: (i) Reserve requirements
children.text:
(i) Reserve requirements
Reserve requirements in respect of the CDs issued by banks shall be governed by relevant
regulations of the Reserve Bank.

children.title: (j) Accounting
children.text:
(j) Accounting
Accounting for CD transactions shall be as per the applicable accounting standards
prescribed by the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI) or other standard
setting organisations or as specified by the relevant regulations of the Reserve Bank.

children.title: (a) Primary issuance
children.text:
(a) Primary issuance
Details of primary issuance of a CD shall be reported by the issuer to the Trade Repository
(TR), i.e., Financial Market Trade Reporting and Confirmation Platform ("F-TRAC") of the
Clearing Corporation of India Ltd. (CCIL) by 5.30 PM on the day of issuance or as decided
by the Reserve Bank from time to time.

children.title: (b) Secondary market transactions
children.text:
(b) Secondary market transactions
All secondary market transactions executed in OTC market and/or on the recognised stock
exchanges in CDs shall be reported, with time stamp, within 15 minutes of execution (the
time when price is agreed) on the F-TRAC platform by each counterparty to the
transaction.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (c) Buyback transactions
children.text:
(c) Buyback transactions
Details of the buyback of a CD shall be reported by the issuer on the F-TRAC platform by

children.title: 5.30 PM on the day of buyback.
children.text: 5.30 PM on the day of buyback.

children.title: (d) Reporting by depositories
children.text:
(d) Reporting by depositories
The depositories shall report to the Reserve Bank, the details of the CDs held with them
in the dematerialised form, in the prescribed format furnished in Annex I, at fortnightly
intervals (on the 15th day and on the last day of the month) and as and when called upon
to do so by the Reserve Bank.

hierarchy.title: 7. Obligation to provide information sought by the Reserve Bank
hierarchy.text:
7. Obligation to provide information sought by the Reserve Bank
The Reserve Bank may call for any information or statement or seek any clarification, which in
the opinion of the Reserve Bank is relevant, from persons or agencies dealing in the CDs,
including eligible issuers/ investors and such persons, agencies and participants shall furnish
the information, statement or clarification.

hierarchy.title: 8. Dissemination of data
hierarchy.text:
8. Dissemination of data
The Reserve Bank or any other person authorised by the Reserve Bank, may publish any
anonymised data related to transactions in primary and secondary markets in CDs.

hierarchy.title: 9. Violation of Directions
hierarchy.text:
9. Violation of Directions
In the event of any person or agency violating any provision of these Directions or the
provisions of any other applicable law, the Reserve Bank may, in addition to taking any penal
or regulatory action in accordance with law, disallow that person or agency from dealing in the
CD market for a period not exceeding one month at a time, after providing reasonable
opportunity to the person or agency to defend its actions, and such action will be made public
by the Reserve Bank.

hierarchy.title: 10. Applicability of other laws, directions, regulations or guidelines
hierarchy.text:
10. Applicability of other laws, directions, regulations or guidelines
Participants in CD market shall abide by the provisions of any directions, regulations or
guidelines issued by any regulator or any other authority that may be applicable, in respect
of issue of or investment in CDs provided that such directions, regulations or guidelines do
not conflict with these Directions. In case of any conflicts, the provisions of these Directions
shall prevail.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

hierarchy.title: 11. These Directions shall apply to the transactions in Certificate of Deposit entered into from the
hierarchy.text:
11. These Directions shall apply to the transactions in Certificate of Deposit entered into from the
date these Directions come into force. Provisions of Section III of FMRD.Master Direction No.
2/2016-17 dated July 07, 2016 shall continue to be applicable to the CDs issued in accordance
with the said Directions till the maturity of those CDs.
